draft.introduction: The Mishkat al-Anwar ("Niche of Lights") is Ghazali's esoteric commentary on the Light Verse of the Quran (24:35). It argues that God alone is the true Light, all other lights being metaphors and reflections.

translation.text: God is the Light of the heavens and the earth. The Real Light is God, and the name "light" for anything else is pure metaphor, possessing no reality. Know that the word "light" may be understood in three senses. First, the common sense: the visible radiance from lamps and stars. Second, the sense of the eye, which is the spirit of perception. Third, the divine sense, which is the Light of lights, the source from which all subsidiary lights are kindled.

translator_notes.note: The Mishkat is Ghazali's most philosophically daring work and the one that most clearly reveals his debt to Neoplatonic emanation theory. His claim that God alone is "real light" pushed the boundaries of orthodox theology. Some scholars regard this text as evidence that Ghazali's public rejection of philosophy concealed a private philosophical spirituality.
